title: Tokens, Source Spans, Document Trees, Invariants, and Traversal · Language Clinic: Visitor Transform
summary: The parser concatenates HTML strings immediately, loses source locations, uses one mutable node class for every construct, and cannot report which input caused invalid nesting. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the visitor transform increment in typed source-mapped document tree. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Traverse and transform nodes without mutating shared input or losing identity, order, diagnostics, and provenance.
Reject cycles, shared mutable children, impossible parent-child pairs, invalid spans, and unbounded depth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Visitor Transform, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to typed source-mapped document tree.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-visitor-transform — Traverse and transform nodes without mutating shared input or losing identity, order, diagnostics, and provenance.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-document-ast-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-document-ast-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_document_ast_3(tokens: int = 18, nodes: int = 15, source_spans: int = 15, invalid_edges: int = 0, max_depth: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or nodes < 1:
        return False, nodes, "tree-shape"
    if source_spans != nodes:
        return False, source_spans, "span-coverage"
    if invalid_edges > 0:
        return False, invalid_edges, "tree-invariant"
    if max_depth < 1 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    assert source_spans == nodes
    return True, nodes, "source-mapped-tree"

    if max_depth < 1 or max_depth > 64:

    assert source_spans == nodes
    return True, nodes, "source-mapped-tree"
